mnemonics: MADD means Move, Add, and Decrement
MAP means Move, Add, and Program
keyPoints: A complete instruction consists of an opcode, operands, and a program counter
The opcode specifies the operation to be performed
The operands are the data being used in the operation
The program counter is used to keep track of the current instruction
The execution of a complete instruction includes fetching, decoding, executing, and storing
Fetch: retrieves the instruction from memory into the instruction register
Decode: decodes the instruction and determines the operation to be performed
Execute: performs the operation specified by the opcode
Store: stores the results of the operation in the appropriate location
